Add high-risk integration tests that validate critical end-to-end system behavior rather than isolated units. Specifically, implement tests that verify the full A/B testing loop by ensuring a user receives a deterministic assignment, the correct UI variant is rendered, analytics events are emitted with the proper variant tagging, and automatic rollback is triggered when performance thresholds are breached. Add tests that simulate checkout failures and confirm that checkout_failure events are emitted and subsequently detected by the anomaly detection system. Ensure idempotency is enforced per user by testing that identical idempotency keys from the same user return the same result while different users do not collide. Implement notification lifecycle tests that cover transitions from pending to failed, followed by retry attempts leading to delivered or terminal failure states, including attempt tracking. Finally, add concurrency tests that simulate multiple simultaneous purchase attempts for the same collectible to ensure overselling does not occur and database-level or application-level locking mechanisms behave correctly.

17		OrderStatusCancelled  OrderStatus = "cancelled"
18	)
19	
20	var AllowedTransitions = map[OrderStatus][]OrderStatus{
21		OrderStatusPending:    {OrderStatusConfirmed, OrderStatusCancelled},
22		OrderStatusConfirmed:  {OrderStatusProcessing, OrderStatusCancelled},
23		OrderStatusProcessing: {OrderStatusCompleted},
24		OrderStatusCompleted:  {},
25		OrderStatusCancelled:  {},
26	}
27	
28	func (s OrderStatus) CanTransitionTo(target OrderStatus) bool {
29		for _, allowed := range AllowedTransitions[s] {
30			if allowed == target {
31				return true
32			}
33		}
34		return false
35	}

114	}

117		return s.listByField(ctx, "seller_id", sellerID, page, pageSize)
118	}
119	
120	func (s *OrderStore) listByField(ctx context.Context, field string, id uuid.UUID, page, pageSize int) ([]model.Order, int, error) {
121		var total int
122		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
123			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE `+field+` = $1`, id).Scan(&total)
124		if err != nil {
125			return nil, 0, err
126		}
127	
128		offset := (page - 1) * pageSize
129		rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
130			`SELECT id, idempotency_key, buyer_id, collectible_id, seller_id, status,
131			        price_snapshot_cents, cancellation_reason, cancelled_by, fulfillment_tracking,
132			        created_at, updated_at
133			 FROM orders WHERE `+field+` = $1
134			 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT $2 OFFSET $3`, id, pageSize, offset)
135		if err != nil {
136			return nil, 0, err
137		}
138		defer rows.Close()
139	
140		var orders []model.Order
141		for rows.Next() {
142			var o model.Order
143			if err := rows.Scan(&o.ID, &o.IdempotencyKey, &o.BuyerID, &o.CollectibleID, &o.SellerID,
144				&o.Status, &o.PriceSnapshotCents, &o.CancellationReason, &o.CancelledBy,
145				&o.FulfillmentTracking, &o.CreatedAt, &o.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
146				return nil, 0, err
147			}
148			orders = append(orders, o)
149		}
150		return orders, total, nil
151	}
152

157			buyerID).Scan(&count)

161	func (s *OrderStore) CountCompletedByBuyer(ctx context.Context, buyerID uuid.UUID) (int, error) {
162		var count int
163		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
164			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE buyer_id = $1 AND status = 'completed'`,
165			buyerID).Scan(&count)
166		return count, err
167	}
168	
169	func (s *OrderStore) CountCancelledInPeriod(ctx context.Context, userID uuid.UUID, hours int) (int, error) {
170		var count int
171		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,
172			`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders
173			 WHERE (buyer_id = $1 OR seller_id = $1)
174			   AND status = 'cancelled'
175			   AND updated_at > NOW() - make_interval(hours => $2)`,
176			userID, hours).Scan(&count)
177		return count, err
178	}
179	
180	func (s *OrderStore) BeginTx(ctx context.Context) (pgx.Tx, error) {
181		return s.pool.Begin(ctx)
182	}
183	
184	func (s *OrderStore) CountByStatus(ctx context.Context) (map[string]int64, error) {
185		rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
186			`SELECT status, COUNT(*) FROM orders GROUP BY status`)
187		if err != nil {
188			return nil, err
189		}
190		defer rows.Close()
191	
192		result := make(map[string]int64)
193		for rows.Next() {
194			var status string
195			var count int64
196			if err := rows.Scan(&status, &count); err != nil {
197				return nil, err
198			}
199			result[status] = count
200		}
201		return result, nil
202	}

30	func (s *AnalyticsStore) GetFunnel(ctx context.Context, days int) (*dto.FunnelResponse, error) {
31		since := time.Now().AddDate(0, 0, -days)
32		var views, orders int64
33		err := s.pool.QueryRow(ctx,

49	func (s *AnalyticsStore) GetRetention(ctx context.Context, days int) ([]dto.RetentionCohort, error) {
50		rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx,
51			`WITH cohorts AS (
52				SELECT user_id, DATE(MIN(created_at)) AS cohort_date
53				FROM analytics_events
54				WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= NOW() - make_interval(days => $1)
55				GROUP BY user_id
56			),
57			activity AS (
58				SELECT DISTINCT user_id, DATE(created_at) AS activity_date
59				FROM analytics_events
60				WHERE user_id IS NOT NULL AND created_at >= NOW() - make_interval(days => $1)
61			)
62			SELECT c.cohort_date::text, COUNT(DISTINCT c.user_id) AS cohort_size,
63			       COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN a.activity_date > c.cohort_date THEN a.user_id END) AS retained
64			FROM cohorts c
65			LEFT JOIN activity a ON c.user_id = a.user_id
66			GROUP BY c.cohort_date
67			ORDER BY c.cohort_date`, days)
68		if err != nil {
69			return nil, err
70		}
71		defer rows.Close()
72	
73		var cohorts []dto.RetentionCohort
74		for rows.Next() {
75			var c dto.RetentionCohort
76			if err := rows.Scan(&c.CohortDate, &c.CohortSize, &c.RetainedCount); err != nil {
77				return nil, err
78			}
79			if c.CohortSize > 0 {
80				c.RetentionRate = float64(c.RetainedCount) / float64(c.CohortSize)
81			}
82			cohorts = append(cohorts, c)
83		}
84		return cohorts, nil
85	}

246		filter := ""

255	
256		var total int

266			string(rune('0'+argIdx)) + ` OFFSET $` + string(rune('0'+argIdx+1))
267		args = append(args, pageSize, offset)
268	
269		rows, err := s.pool.Query(ctx, query, args...)
270		if err != nil {

110	# A/B Variant Assignment Determinism
111	# =============================================
112	def fnv32a(data: bytes) -> int:
113	    """Python implementation of FNV-1a 32-bit hash matching Go's hash/fnv."""
114	    FNV_PRIME = 0x01000193
115	    FNV_OFFSET = 0x811C9DC5
116	    h = FNV_OFFSET
117	    for b in data:
118	        h ^= b
119	        h = (h * FNV_PRIME) & 0xFFFFFFFF
120	    return h
121	
122	
123	def assign_variant(user_id: str, test_name: str, traffic_pct: int) -> str:
124	    h = fnv32a((user_id + test_name).encode())
125	    bucket = h % 100
126	    return "test" if bucket < traffic_pct else "control"
127	
128

138	        results = set()
139	        for i in range(100):
140	            results.add(assign_variant(f"user{i}", "test1", 50))
141	        self.assertEqual(results, {"test", "control"})

### 2. A/B TESTING SYSTEM

**Models** (`/backend/internal/model/abtest.go`):
- `ABTest`: name, description, status (draft/running/rolled_back/completed), traffic_pct (1-100), start/end dates, control/test variant names, rollback threshold %, creator
- `ABTestResult`: ab_test_id, variant, views, orders, conversion_rate, computed_at

**Service Logic** (`/backend/internal/service/abtest_service.go`):
- **Assignment**: `AssignVariant()` uses **deterministic FNV-1a 32-bit hashing** of `userID+testName`, mod 100 against `traffic_pct`
  - Returns "test" or "control" variant
  - **Deterministic**: same user always gets same variant for same test
- **Lifecycle**: Create → Auto-start if start_date is past → Running → Rollback/Completed
- **Rollback**: Manual rollback via `Rollback()` or automatic via `ABTestEvaluatorJob` if relative conversion drop > threshold % with minimum 100 views per variant

**Analytics Events** (`/backend/internal/model/analytics.go`):
- Captured with `ABVariant` field: `"testName:variantName"` (e.g., "test1:variant_a")
- Event types: view, item_view, catalog_view, order_created, order_cancelled, checkout_failed

**Worker Job** (`/backend/internal/worker/abtest_evaluator.go`):
- Runs every 5 minutes
- Checks if test has ended, updates to completed
- Computes conversion rates (views → orders) for control & test variants
- Auto-rollback: if control > 0 AND (control-test)/control*100 > threshold % AND both have ≥100 views, auto-rollback

**Database Schema** (Migration 006):
```sql
ab_tests: id, name (UNIQUE), description, status, traffic_pct (1-100), start_date, end_date, 
          control_variant, test_variant, rollback_threshold_pct, created_by
ab_test_results: id, ab_test_id, variant, views, orders, conversion_rate, computed_at
```

---

### 3. CHECKOUT FLOW

**Order Creation** (`/backend/internal/service/order_service.go::Create()`):
1. **Idempotency Check**: Query `orders` table by `idempotency_key` - if exists, return existing order immediately
2. **Validation**:
   - Collectible exists and status='published'
   - Buyer ≠ Seller (prevents self-purchase)
   - Emit `checkout_failed` event on validation errors
3. **Concurrency Protection** (PostgreSQL advisory locks):
   - `BEGIN TRANSACTION`
   - `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(collectibleID::text))` - transaction-scoped lock
   - Query: active orders where `status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'completed')` on this collectible
   - If active order exists → return `ERR_OVERSOLD` (409)
4. **Order Creation**:
   - Insert with status='pending', idempotency_key (UNIQUE constraint), price snapshot
   - Record state transition: → pending
   - `COMMIT`
5. **Post-Creation**:
   - Emit `order_created` event with A/B variant assignment
   - Return order

**State Transitions** (`/backend/internal/model/order.go`):
```
pending    → [confirmed, cancelled]
confirmed  → [processing, cancelled]
processing → [completed]
completed  → [terminal]
cancelled  → [terminal]
```

**Handlers** (`/backend/internal/handler/order_handler.go`):
- POST `/api/orders` - Create (requires Idempotency-Key header, buyer role)
- POST `/api/orders/{id}/confirm` - Confirm (seller only)
- POST `/api/orders/{id}/process` - Process (seller only)
- POST `/api/orders/{id}/complete` - Complete (seller only)
- POST `/api/orders/{id}/cancel` - Cancel (buyer or seller)
- PATCH `/api/orders/{id}/fulfillment` - Update tracking (seller only)

**Failure Handling**:
- Overselling: 409 ERR_OVERSOLD
- Invalid transitions: 422 ERR_INVALID_TRANSITION
- Object-level authorization: 403 ErrForbidden
- Analytics events: `checkout_failed` for validation failures

**Anomaly Detection** (`/backend/internal/worker/anomaly_detector.go`):
- Runs every 5 minutes
- Detects: >6 cancellations in 24h, >10 checkout_failed events in 1h per user
- Creates `AnomalyEvent` records for compliance review

---

### 4. IDEMPOTENCY

**Implementation** (`/backend/internal/service/order_service.go`):
- **Mechanism**: Unique constraint on `orders.idempotency_key` column
- **Key Requirement**: Client must provide `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header
- **Enforcement**: Per-user enforcement implicit via transaction scope
  - Key is globally unique; same key from any user returns same order
  - For true per-user idempotency, keys could include user prefix (current implementation is global)
- **Lookup**: `GetByIdempotencyKey()` before creation
- **Error**: Missing header → 400 ERR_VALIDATION

**Database**:
```sql
CREATE TABLE orders (
  idempotency_key VARCHAR(64) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  ...
);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_idempotency ON orders(idempotency_key);
```

**API Contract**:
- Header: `Idempotency-Key: <uuid>`
- Response: Same order returned for same key, HTTP 201 for first request, 200 for retries (implementation uses 201 both times)

---

### 5. NOTIFICATION SYSTEM

**Models** (`/backend/internal/model/notification.go`):
- `Notification`: user_id, template_id, params (JSON), rendered_title, rendered_body, is_read, status, retry_count, max_retries, next_retry_at, delivered_at
- `NotificationTemplate`: id, slug (UNIQUE), title_template, body_template
- `NotificationPreferences`: user_id, preferences (JSON map of slug → bool)

**Lifecycle States** (Migration 009):
```
pending → [delivered, failed]
failed → [delivered, permanently_failed]
delivered → [terminal]
permanently_failed → [terminal]
```

**Retry Logic** (`/backend/internal/worker/notification_retry.go`):
- **Pending Delivery** (runs every 60s):
  - `GetPendingForDelivery(limit=100)` - status='pending' notifications
  - Mark as 'delivered' with `delivered_at=NOW()`
- **Failed Retry** (same job):
  - `GetFailedForRetry(limit=50)` - status='failed' AND retry_count < max_retries AND (next_retry_at IS NULL OR next_retry_at ≤ NOW())
  - **Exponential Backoff**: nextRetry = now + (1 << newRetryCount) minutes
    - Retry 1: 2 min
    - Retry 2: 4 min
    - Retry 3: 8 min
  - After max_retries (default=3): status='permanently_failed'

**Service** (`/backend/internal/service/notification_service.go`):
- `Send()`: Check preferences → fetch template → render (simple {{.Key}} substitution) → insert with status='pending'
- `MarkRead()`, `MarkAllRead()`, `CountUnread()`: Read state management
- `Retry()`: Manual retry (user-initiated) on failed notifications
- `UpdatePreferences()`: Per-user opt-in/opt-out by template slug

**Templates** (seeded in Migration 005):
- order_confirmed, order_processing, order_completed, order_cancelled
- refund_approved, arbitration_opened, review_posted

**Handlers** (`/backend/internal/handler/notification_handler.go`):
- GET `/api/notifications` - List (with ?unread=true, ?count=true, pagination)
- PATCH `/api/notifications/{id}/read` - Mark read
- POST `/api/notifications/read-all` - Mark all read
- POST `/api/notifications/{id}/retry` - Manual retry
- GET/PUT `/api/notifications/preferences` - Get/update preferences

**Database**:
```sql
CREATE TABLE notifications (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id UUID NOT NULL,
  template_id UUID NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'delivered', 'failed', 'permanently_failed')),
  retry_count INT DEFAULT 0,
  max_retries INT DEFAULT 3,
  next_retry_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  ...
);
CREATE INDEX idx_notifications_retry ON notifications(status, next_retry_at) 
  WHERE status = 'failed' AND retry_count < max_retries;
```

---

### 6. CONCURRENCY & LOCKING

**Database-Level Locking**:
- **PostgreSQL Advisory Locks** (transaction-scoped):
  - Used in `order_service.Create()` to prevent overselling
  - `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(collectibleID::text))` during transaction
  - Lock released automatically on COMMIT/ROLLBACK

**Transaction Management**:
- **Order Store** (`/backend/internal/store/order_store.go`):
  - `BeginTx()` - Start transaction
  - `AcquireAdvisoryLock()` - Acquire lock within transaction
  - `HasActiveOrder()` - Check for active orders (within tx)
  - `CreateInTx()` - Insert order (within tx)
  - `RecordTransitionInTx()` - Record state change (within tx)
  - Auto-rollback on error via deferred `tx.Rollback()`

**Race Condition Prevention**:
- Idempotency key uniqueness prevents duplicate orders
- Advisory lock prevents concurrent order attempts on same collectible
- Minimal transaction scope (lock → check → insert → release)

**Connection Pooling**: pgx/v5 connection pool (`pgxpool.Pool`)

**Potential Concurrency Issues**:
- Notification retry job: concurrent attempts to retry same notification could cause duplicate deliveries (no lock in `UpdateRetryState`)
- A/B test evaluation: concurrent reads safe, status updates via `UpdateABTestStatus` could race (last-write-wins)

### 9. TESTING BEST PRACTICES OBSERVED

1. **Deterministic A/B Assignment**: Same user always gets same variant (FNV-1a hash)
2. **State Machine Validation**: Order transitions validated in model layer before persistence
3. **Anomaly Detection**: Separate worker job monitors business rule violations
4. **Append-Only History**: Transaction history immutable via database trigger
5. **Authorization**: Object-level checks (buyer/seller of order, own notifications)
6. **Idempotency**: Global unique key with database constraint
7. **Exponential Backoff**: Notification retries with exponential delay

This codebase is well-structured for integration testing with clear separation of concerns, comprehensive data models, and explicit state machines.

## THOROUGH REPOSITORY EXPLORATION REPORT

I've completed an in-depth analysis of the repository. Here's what I found:

### 1. TEST STRUCTURE

**Framework & Organization:**
- **Unit Tests**: Python-based (pytest/unittest) in `/Users/macbookpro/Projects/eaglepoint/w1t21/repo/unit_tests/`
  - `test_error_codes.py` - Error code validation
  - `test_formatters.py` - Data formatting tests
  - `test_new_behaviors.py` - Production behavior validation (collectible identity, notification lifecycle, A/B assignment determinism, analytics events, dashboard metrics)
  - `test_order_state_machine.py` - Order state machine validation
  - `test_pii_detection.py` - PII detection tests
  - `test_validation.py` - Input validation tests

- **API Integration Tests**: Bash/curl-based in `/Users/macbookpro/Projects/eaglepoint/w1t21/repo/API_tests/`
  - `test_00_setup.sh` - Test setup and teardown
  - `test_auth.sh` - Authentication flows
  - `test_collectibles.sh` - Collectible CRUD and listing
  - `test_e2e_hardening.sh` - End-to-end production hardening (A/B tests, checkout failures, overselling protection, collectible identity validation, dashboard metrics)
  - `test_messages.sh` - Messaging system
  - `test_notifications.sh` - Notification lifecycle and preferences
  - `test_orders.sh` - Order CRUD, idempotency, state transitions
  - `test_security.sh` - Security hardening

**Backend Technology**: Go (Echo framework) with PostgreSQL
- No native Go test files found (tests are external integration/unit tests)

---

### 2. A/B TESTING SYSTEM

**Models** (`/backend/internal/model/abtest.go`):
- `ABTest`: name, description, status (draft/running/rolled_back/completed), traffic_pct (1-100), start/end dates, control/test variant names, rollback threshold %, creator
- `ABTestResult`: ab_test_id, variant, views, orders, conversion_rate, computed_at

**Service Logic** (`/backend/internal/service/abtest_service.go`):
- **Assignment**: `AssignVariant()` uses **deterministic FNV-1a 32-bit hashing** of `userID+testName`, mod 100 against `traffic_pct`
  - Returns "test" or "control" variant
  - **Deterministic**: same user always gets same variant for same test
- **Lifecycle**: Create → Auto-start if start_date is past → Running → Rollback/Completed
- **Rollback**: Manual rollback via `Rollback()` or automatic via `ABTestEvaluatorJob` if relative conversion drop > threshold % with minimum 100 views per variant

**Analytics Events** (`/backend/internal/model/analytics.go`):
- Captured with `ABVariant` field: `"testName:variantName"` (e.g., "test1:variant_a")
- Event types: view, item_view, catalog_view, order_created, order_cancelled, checkout_failed

**Worker Job** (`/backend/internal/worker/abtest_evaluator.go`):
- Runs every 5 minutes
- Checks if test has ended, updates to completed
- Computes conversion rates (views → orders) for control & test variants
- Auto-rollback: if control > 0 AND (control-test)/control*100 > threshold % AND both have ≥100 views, auto-rollback

**Database Schema** (Migration 006):
```sql
ab_tests: id, name (UNIQUE), description, status, traffic_pct (1-100), start_date, end_date, 
          control_variant, test_variant, rollback_threshold_pct, created_by
ab_test_results: id, ab_test_id, variant, views, orders, conversion_rate, computed_at
```

---

### 3. CHECKOUT FLOW

**Order Creation** (`/backend/internal/service/order_service.go::Create()`):
1. **Idempotency Check**: Query `orders` table by `idempotency_key` - if exists, return existing order immediately
2. **Validation**:
   - Collectible exists and status='published'
   - Buyer ≠ Seller (prevents self-purchase)
   - Emit `checkout_failed` event on validation errors
3. **Concurrency Protection** (PostgreSQL advisory locks):
   - `BEGIN TRANSACTION`
   - `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(collectibleID::text))` - transaction-scoped lock
   - Query: active orders where `status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'completed')` on this collectible
   - If active order exists → return `ERR_OVERSOLD` (409)
4. **Order Creation**:
   - Insert with status='pending', idempotency_key (UNIQUE constraint), price snapshot
   - Record state transition: → pending
   - `COMMIT`
5. **Post-Creation**:
   - Emit `order_created` event with A/B variant assignment
   - Return order

**State Transitions** (`/backend/internal/model/order.go`):
```
pending    → [confirmed, cancelled]
confirmed  → [processing, cancelled]
processing → [completed]
completed  → [terminal]
cancelled  → [terminal]
```

**Handlers** (`/backend/internal/handler/order_handler.go`):
- POST `/api/orders` - Create (requires Idempotency-Key header, buyer role)
- POST `/api/orders/{id}/confirm` - Confirm (seller only)
- POST `/api/orders/{id}/process` - Process (seller only)
- POST `/api/orders/{id}/complete` - Complete (seller only)
- POST `/api/orders/{id}/cancel` - Cancel (buyer or seller)
- PATCH `/api/orders/{id}/fulfillment` - Update tracking (seller only)

**Failure Handling**:
- Overselling: 409 ERR_OVERSOLD
- Invalid transitions: 422 ERR_INVALID_TRANSITION
- Object-level authorization: 403 ErrForbidden
- Analytics events: `checkout_failed` for validation failures

**Anomaly Detection** (`/backend/internal/worker/anomaly_detector.go`):
- Runs every 5 minutes
- Detects: >6 cancellations in 24h, >10 checkout_failed events in 1h per user
- Creates `AnomalyEvent` records for compliance review

---

### 4. IDEMPOTENCY

**Implementation** (`/backend/internal/service/order_service.go`):
- **Mechanism**: Unique constraint on `orders.idempotency_key` column
- **Key Requirement**: Client must provide `Idempotency-Key` HTTP header
- **Enforcement**: Per-user enforcement implicit via transaction scope
  - Key is globally unique; same key from any user returns same order
  - For true per-user idempotency, keys could include user prefix (current implementation is global)
- **Lookup**: `GetByIdempotencyKey()` before creation
- **Error**: Missing header → 400 ERR_VALIDATION

**Database**:
```sql
CREATE TABLE orders (
  idempotency_key VARCHAR(64) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  ...
);
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_idempotency ON orders(idempotency_key);
```

**API Contract**:
- Header: `Idempotency-Key: <uuid>`
- Response: Same order returned for same key, HTTP 201 for first request, 200 for retries (implementation uses 201 both times)

---

### 5. NOTIFICATION SYSTEM

**Models** (`/backend/internal/model/notification.go`):
- `Notification`: user_id, template_id, params (JSON), rendered_title, rendered_body, is_read, status, retry_count, max_retries, next_retry_at, delivered_at
- `NotificationTemplate`: id, slug (UNIQUE), title_template, body_template
- `NotificationPreferences`: user_id, preferences (JSON map of slug → bool)

**Lifecycle States** (Migration 009):
```
pending → [delivered, failed]
failed → [delivered, permanently_failed]
delivered → [terminal]
permanently_failed → [terminal]
```

**Retry Logic** (`/backend/internal/worker/notification_retry.go`):
- **Pending Delivery** (runs every 60s):
  - `GetPendingForDelivery(limit=100)` - status='pending' notifications
  - Mark as 'delivered' with `delivered_at=NOW()`
- **Failed Retry** (same job):
  - `GetFailedForRetry(limit=50)` - status='failed' AND retry_count < max_retries AND (next_retry_at IS NULL OR next_retry_at ≤ NOW())
  - **Exponential Backoff**: nextRetry = now + (1 << newRetryCount) minutes
    - Retry 1: 2 min
    - Retry 2: 4 min
    - Retry 3: 8 min
  - After max_retries (default=3): status='permanently_failed'

**Service** (`/backend/internal/service/notification_service.go`):
- `Send()`: Check preferences → fetch template → render (simple {{.Key}} substitution) → insert with status='pending'
- `MarkRead()`, `MarkAllRead()`, `CountUnread()`: Read state management
- `Retry()`: Manual retry (user-initiated) on failed notifications
- `UpdatePreferences()`: Per-user opt-in/opt-out by template slug

**Templates** (seeded in Migration 005):
- order_confirmed, order_processing, order_completed, order_cancelled
- refund_approved, arbitration_opened, review_posted

**Handlers** (`/backend/internal/handler/notification_handler.go`):
- GET `/api/notifications` - List (with ?unread=true, ?count=true, pagination)
- PATCH `/api/notifications/{id}/read` - Mark read
- POST `/api/notifications/read-all` - Mark all read
- POST `/api/notifications/{id}/retry` - Manual retry
- GET/PUT `/api/notifications/preferences` - Get/update preferences

**Database**:
```sql
CREATE TABLE notifications (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id UUID NOT NULL,
  template_id UUID NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'delivered', 'failed', 'permanently_failed')),
  retry_count INT DEFAULT 0,
  max_retries INT DEFAULT 3,
  next_retry_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  delivered_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  ...
);
CREATE INDEX idx_notifications_retry ON notifications(status, next_retry_at) 
  WHERE status = 'failed' AND retry_count < max_retries;
```

---

### 6. CONCURRENCY & LOCKING

**Database-Level Locking**:
- **PostgreSQL Advisory Locks** (transaction-scoped):
  - Used in `order_service.Create()` to prevent overselling
  - `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(collectibleID::text))` during transaction
  - Lock released automatically on COMMIT/ROLLBACK

**Transaction Management**:
- **Order Store** (`/backend/internal/store/order_store.go`):
  - `BeginTx()` - Start transaction
  - `AcquireAdvisoryLock()` - Acquire lock within transaction
  - `HasActiveOrder()` - Check for active orders (within tx)
  - `CreateInTx()` - Insert order (within tx)
  - `RecordTransitionInTx()` - Record state change (within tx)
  - Auto-rollback on error via deferred `tx.Rollback()`

**Race Condition Prevention**:
- Idempotency key uniqueness prevents duplicate orders
- Advisory lock prevents concurrent order attempts on same collectible
- Minimal transaction scope (lock → check → insert → release)

**Connection Pooling**: pgx/v5 connection pool (`pgxpool.Pool`)

**Potential Concurrency Issues**:
- Notification retry job: concurrent attempts to retry same notification could cause duplicate deliveries (no lock in `UpdateRetryState`)
- A/B test evaluation: concurrent reads safe, status updates via `UpdateABTestStatus` could race (last-write-wins)

---

### 7. DATABASE MODELS & SCHEMA

**Core Tables** (ordered by migration):

**001 - Users & Roles**:
```sql
users: id, username, password_hash, display_name, email, is_locked, created_at
user_roles: user_id, role (buyer/seller/administrator/compliance_analyst)
```

**002 - Collectibles**:
```sql
collectibles: id, seller_id, title, description, contract_address, chain_id, token_id, 
              metadata_uri, image_url, price_cents, currency, status (draft/published/hidden), 
              created_at, updated_at
collectible_tx_history: id, collectible_id, event_type, details, created_at [APPEND-ONLY]
```
- Unique index on (contract_address, chain_id, token_id) where contract_address != ''
- Validation: contract_address requires chain_id AND token_id

**003 - Orders**:
```sql
orders: id, idempotency_key (UNIQUE), buyer_id, collectible_id, seller_id, 
        status (pending/confirmed/processing/completed/cancelled), price_snapshot_cents, 
        cancellation_reason, cancelled_by, fulfillment_tracking (JSONB), created_at, updated_at
order_state_transitions: id, order_id, from_status, to_status, actor_id, reason, created_at
```
- Indexes: idempotency_key, buyer_id+created_at, seller_id+created_at, collectible_id (active only)

**004 - Messages**:
```sql
messages: id, order_id, sender_id, body, attachment_url, created_at
```

**005 - Notifications**:
```sql
notification_templates: id, slug (UNIQUE), title_template, body_template, created_at
notifications: id, user_id, template_id, params (JSONB), rendered_title, rendered_body, 
               is_read, status (pending/delivered/failed/permanently_failed), retry_count, 
               max_retries, next_retry_at, delivered_at, created_at
notification_preferences: user_id (PRIMARY), preferences (JSONB)
```
- Indexes: user_id+is_read+created_at, status+next_retry_at (for failed/retryable)

**006 - Analytics & A/B Tests**:
```sql
analytics_events: id, user_id, event_type, collectible_id, session_id, ab_variant, 
                  metadata (JSONB), created_at
ab_tests: id, name (UNIQUE), description, status (draft/running/rolled_back/completed), 
          traffic_pct, start_date, end_date, control_variant, test_variant, 
          rollback_threshold_pct, created_by, created_at, updated_at
ab_test_results: id, ab_test_id, variant, views, orders, conversion_rate, computed_at
anomaly_events: id, user_id, anomaly_type, details (JSONB), acknowledged, created_at
```
- Indexes: event_type+created_at, user_id+created_at, collectible_id, ab_test_id+computed_at, anomaly_acknowledged+created_at

**008 - Production Hardening**:
- Unique index on collectible identity (contract_address, chain_id, token_id)
- Indexes for analytics event queries

**009 - Notification States**:
- ALTER notifications status CHECK constraint to include 'permanently_failed'

**010 - Transaction History Immutability**:
- Trigger `prevent_tx_history_mutation()` blocks UPDATE/DELETE on collectible_tx_history

---

### 8. KEY FILES FOR INTEGRATION TESTING

**Critical Service Files**:
- `/backend/internal/service/order_service.go` - Order creation with idempotency & locking
- `/backend/internal/service/notification_service.go` - Notification lifecycle
- `/backend/internal/service/abtest_service.go` - A/B assignment & variant logic
- `/backend/internal/store/order_store.go` - Transaction management, advisory locks
- `/backend/internal/store/notification_store.go` - Notification persistence & retry queries
- `/backend/internal/store/analytics_store.go` - Event recording, A/B test queries

**Handler Files** (API contracts):
- `/backend/internal/handler/order_handler.go`
- `/backend/internal/handler/notification_handler.go`
- `/backend/internal/handler/abtest_handler.go`
- `/backend/internal/handler/analytics_handler.go`

**Models** (Data structures):
- `/backend/internal/model/order.go` - Order, OrderStateTransition, status transitions
- `/backend/internal/model/notification.go` - Notification, NotificationTemplate, NotificationPreferences
- `/backend/internal/model/abtest.go` - ABTest, ABTestResult, status transitions
- `/backend/internal/model/analytics.go` - AnalyticsEvent, AnomalyEvent

**Worker Jobs** (Background processing):
- `/backend/internal/worker/notification_retry.go` - Notification delivery & retry
- `/backend/internal/worker/anomaly_detector.go` - Anomaly detection rules
- `/backend/internal/worker/abtest_evaluator.go` - A/B test auto-rollback
- `/backend/internal/worker/scheduler.go` - Job scheduling framework

**Migrations** (Schema contracts):
- `/backend/migrations/003_orders.up.sql` - Order schema, advisory lock usage
- `/backend/migrations/005_notifications.up.sql` - Notification states, templates
- `/backend/migrations/006_analytics_and_abtests.up.sql` - A/B test & event schema
- `/backend/migrations/008_production_hardening.up.sql` - Production indexes
- `/backend/migrations/009_notification_states.up.sql` - Full notification lifecycle
- `/backend/migrations/010_tx_history_immutable.up.sql` - Append-only constraints

**Test References**:
- `/unit_tests/test_order_state_machine.py` - State machine validation
- `/unit_tests/test_new_behaviors.py` - Notification lifecycle, A/B assignment, analytics taxonomy
- `/API_tests/test_e2e_hardening.sh` - End-to-end checkout, overselling, A/B tests
- `/API_tests/test_orders.sh` - Order API & idempotency
- `/API_tests/test_notifications.sh` - Notification API & preferences

---

### 9. TESTING BEST PRACTICES OBSERVED

1. **Deterministic A/B Assignment**: Same user always gets same variant (FNV-1a hash)
2. **State Machine Validation**: Order transitions validated in model layer before persistence
3. **Anomaly Detection**: Separate worker job monitors business rule violations
4. **Append-Only History**: Transaction history immutable via database trigger
5. **Authorization**: Object-level checks (buyer/seller of order, own notifications)
6. **Idempotency**: Global unique key with database constraint
7. **Exponential Backoff**: Notification retries with exponential delay

This codebase is well-structured for integration testing with clear separation of concerns, comprehensive data models, and explicit state machines.

112	def fnv32a(data: bytes) -> int:
113	    """Python implementation of FNV-1a 32-bit hash matching Go's hash/fnv."""
114	    FNV_PRIME = 0x01000193
115	    FNV_OFFSET = 0x811C9DC5
116	    h = FNV_OFFSET
117	    for b in data:
118	        h ^= b
119	        h = (h * FNV_PRIME) & 0xFFFFFFFF
120	    return h
121

123	def assign_variant(user_id: str, test_name: str, traffic_pct: int) -> str:
124	    h = fnv32a((user_id + test_name).encode())
125	    bucket = h % 100
126	    return "test" if bucket < traffic_pct else "control"

20	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected HTTP $expected, got HTTP $actual)"
21	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
22	    fi
23	}
24	
25	assert_json() {
26	    local test_name="$1" body="$2" expr="$3" expected="$4"
27	    local actual
28	    actual=$(echo "$body" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print($expr)" 2>/dev/null)
29	    if [ "$actual" = "$expected" ]; then

32	    else

37	
38	login_as() {
39	    local user="$1" jar="$2"
40	    curl -s -c "$jar" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
41	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
42	        -d "{\"username\":\"$user\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}" > /dev/null
43	}
44	get_csrf() { grep csrf_token "$1" | awk '{print $NF}'; }
45	
46	ADMIN_JAR=$(mktemp); SELLER_JAR=$(mktemp); BUYER_JAR=$(mktemp)
47	login_as "admin" "$ADMIN_JAR"; ADMIN_CSRF=$(get_csrf "$ADMIN_JAR")
48	login_as "seller1" "$SELLER_JAR"; SELLER_CSRF=$(get_csrf "$SELLER_JAR")
49	login_as "buyer1" "$BUYER_JAR"; BUYER_CSRF=$(get_csrf "$BUYER_JAR")
50	cleanup() { rm -f "$ADMIN_JAR" "$SELLER_JAR" "$BUYER_JAR"; }
51	trap cleanup EXIT
52	
53	echo "=== E2E Hardening Tests ==="
54	
55	# =============================================
56	# 1. Collectible Identity Validation
57	# =============================================
58	echo ""
59	echo "--- Collectible Identity ---"
60	
61	# contract_address without chain_id/token_id should fail
62	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
63	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
64	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
65	    -d '{"title":"Bad Identity","price_cents":1000,"contract_address":"0xabc"}')
66	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
67	assert_status "contract_address without chain_id/token_id rejected (422)" "422" "$HTTP_CODE"
68	
69	# With all three identity fields should succeed
70	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
71	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
72	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
73	    -d '{"title":"Full Identity NFT","price_cents":5000,"contract_address":"0x1111111111111111111111111111111111111111","chain_id":137,"token_id":"unique-tok-001"}')
74	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
75	assert_status "Full identity collectible created (201)" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
76	
77	# Duplicate identity should fail (409)
78	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
79	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
80	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
81	    -d '{"title":"Duplicate NFT","price_cents":5000,"contract_address":"0x1111111111111111111111111111111111111111","chain_id":137,"token_id":"unique-tok-001"}')
82	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
83	assert_status "Duplicate collectible identity rejected (409)" "409" "$HTTP_CODE"
84	
85	# Without contract_address, chain_id and token_id auto-generated
86	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
87	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
88	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
89	    -d '{"title":"Auto Identity","price_cents":2000}')
90	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
91	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
92	assert_status "Auto-identity collectible created (201)" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
93	AUTO_CHAIN=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('chain_id',0))" 2>/dev/null)
94	AUTO_TOKEN=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token_id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
95	if [ "$AUTO_CHAIN" = "1" ] && [ -n "$AUTO_TOKEN" ] && [ "$AUTO_TOKEN" != "" ]; then
96	    echo "  PASS: Auto-generated chain_id=1 and token_id ($AUTO_TOKEN)"
97	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
98	else
99	    echo "  FAIL: Expected auto-generated chain_id=1 and non-empty token_id (got chain=$AUTO_CHAIN token=$AUTO_TOKEN)"
100	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
101	fi
102	
103	# =============================================
104	# 2. Checkout Failure Events (overselling)
105	# =============================================
106	echo ""
107	echo "--- Checkout Failure Events ---"
108	
109	# Create a collectible and place first order
110	IDEM1=$(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")
111	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
112	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
113	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
114	    -d '{"title":"Oversell Test","price_cents":3000}')
115	OVERSELL_ID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
116	
117	if [ -n "$OVERSELL_ID" ] && [ "$OVERSELL_ID" != "" ] && [ "$OVERSELL_ID" != "None" ]; then
118	    # First order succeeds
119	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
120	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
121	        -H "Idempotency-Key: $IDEM1" \
122	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders" \
123	        -d "{\"collectible_id\":\"$OVERSELL_ID\"}")
124	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
125	    assert_status "First order succeeds (201)" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
126	
127	    # Second order on same item should fail with oversold (409)
128	    IDEM2=$(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")
129	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
130	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
131	        -H "Idempotency-Key: $IDEM2" \
132	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders" \
133	        -d "{\"collectible_id\":\"$OVERSELL_ID\"}")
134	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
135	    BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
136	    assert_status "Second order rejected as oversold (409)" "409" "$HTTP_CODE"
137	    assert_json "Error code is ERR_OVERSOLD" "$BODY" "d['error']['code']" "ERR_OVERSOLD"
138	fi
139	
140	# =============================================
141	# 3. A/B Test → Assignment → Event Tagging
142	# =============================================
143	echo ""
144	echo "--- A/B Test End-to-End ---"
145	
146	# Create a running A/B test
147	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
148	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
149	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests" \
150	    -d '{
151	        "name":"e2e_test","description":"End to end test",
152	        "traffic_pct":100,"start_date":"2020-01-01T00:00","end_date":"2099-12-31T23:59",
153	        "control_variant":"default","test_variant":"experimental","rollback_threshold_pct":50
154	    }')
155	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
156	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
157	assert_status "Create running A/B test (201)" "201" "$HTTP_CODE"
158	AB_TEST_ID=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
159	
160	# Get assignments as buyer — should include the test
161	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
162	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
163	    "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests/assignments")
164	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
165	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
166	assert_status "Get A/B assignments (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
167	
168	HAS_E2E=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "
169	import sys,json
170	data=json.load(sys.stdin)
171	found=any(a['test_name']=='e2e_test' for a in data)
172	print(found)
173	" 2>/dev/null)
174	if [ "$HAS_E2E" = "True" ]; then
175	    echo "  PASS: Buyer assigned to e2e_test"
176	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
177	else
178	    echo "  FAIL: Buyer should be assigned to e2e_test"
179	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
180	fi
181	
182	# Trigger a view event (which should be tagged with the A/B variant)
183	curl -s -b "$BUYER_JAR" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
184	    "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles/10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001" > /dev/null
185	sleep 1
186	
187	# Rollback the test
188	if [ -n "$AB_TEST_ID" ] && [ "$AB_TEST_ID" != "None" ]; then
189	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
190	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
191	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests/$AB_TEST_ID/rollback")
192	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
193	    assert_status "Rollback A/B test (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
194	
195	    # Verify it's rolled back — can't rollback again
196	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$ADMIN_JAR" \
197	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $ADMIN_CSRF" \
198	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/ab-tests/$AB_TEST_ID/rollback")
199	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
200	    assert_status "Double rollback fails (422)" "422" "$HTTP_CODE"
201	fi
202	
203	# =============================================
204	# 4. Notification Lifecycle
205	# =============================================
206	echo ""
207	echo "--- Notification Lifecycle ---"
208	
209	# Trigger a notification by confirming an order
210	NOTIF_IDEM=$(python3 -c "import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())")
211	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
212	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
213	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" \
214	    -d '{"title":"Notif Test Item","price_cents":1000}')
215	NOTIF_CID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
216	
217	if [ -n "$NOTIF_CID" ] && [ "$NOTIF_CID" != "None" ]; then
218	    # Buyer places order
219	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
220	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
221	        -H "Idempotency-Key: $NOTIF_IDEM" \
222	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders" \
223	        -d "{\"collectible_id\":\"$NOTIF_CID\"}")
224	    NOTIF_OID=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('id',''))" 2>/dev/null)
225	
226	    if [ -n "$NOTIF_OID" ] && [ "$NOTIF_OID" != "None" ]; then
227	        # Seller confirms — triggers notification to buyer
228	        curl -s -b "$SELLER_JAR" \
229	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "X-CSRF-Token: $SELLER_CSRF" \
230	            -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/orders/$NOTIF_OID/confirm" > /dev/null
231	
232	        sleep 1
233	
234	        # Check buyer has notifications
235	        RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
236	            -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
237	            "$BASE_URL/api/notifications")
238	        HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
239	        BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
240	        assert_status "Buyer has notifications (200)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
241	
242	        NOTIF_COUNT=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('total_count',0))" 2>/dev/null)
243	        if [ "$NOTIF_COUNT" -gt 0 ] 2>/dev/null; then
244	            echo "  PASS: Buyer has $NOTIF_COUNT notification(s)"
245	            PASS=$((PASS + 1))
246	        else
247	            echo "  FAIL: Buyer should have notifications after order confirmed"
248	            FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
249	        fi
250	    fi
251	fi
252	
253	# =============================================
254	# 5. Dashboard Owned Items = Completed Purchases
255	# =============================================
256	echo ""
257	echo "--- Dashboard Owned Items ---"
258	
259	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$BUYER_JAR" \
260	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $BUYER_CSRF" \
261	    "$BASE_URL/api/dashboard")
262	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
263	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
264	assert_status "Dashboard returns 200" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
265	
266	# owned_collectibles should reflect completed orders (not seller listings)
267	HAS_FIELD=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print('owned_collectibles' in d)" 2>/dev/null)
268	if [ "$HAS_FIELD" = "True" ]; then
269	    echo "  PASS: Dashboard includes owned_collectibles field"
270	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
271	else
272	    echo "  FAIL: Dashboard should include owned_collectibles"
273	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
274	fi
275	
276	# =============================================
277	# 6. Encrypted Config Support
278	# =============================================
279	echo ""
280	echo "--- Config Security ---"
281	# We can't test the actual encrypted keyfile loading at runtime since the
282	# server is already running with env vars. But we verify the code accepts
283	# development mode by checking the /api/auth/login endpoint still works
284	# (proving the config loaded successfully with plaintext env in dev mode).
285	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
286	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
287	    -d '{"username":"admin","password":"testpass123"}')
288	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
289	assert_status "Server running with config (login works)" "200" "$HTTP_CODE"
290	
291	echo ""
292	echo "=== E2E Hardening Tests Summary: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed ==="
293	exit $FAIL

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Pre-flight check: verifies API is reachable and seed data is present.
3	# This script runs first (sorted alphabetically) so the suite fails
4	# immediately with a clear message instead of producing confusing
5	# 401/403 noise when seed users or collectibles are missing.
6	
7	BASE_URL="${API_BASE_URL:-http://localhost:8080}"
8	PASS=0
9	FAIL=0
10	
11	assert_status() {
12	    local test_name="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3"
13	    if [ "$expected" = "$actual" ]; then
14	        echo "  PASS: $test_name (HTTP $actual)"
15	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
16	    else
17	        echo "  FAIL: $test_name (expected HTTP $expected, got HTTP $actual)"
18	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
19	    fi
20	}
21	
22	echo "=== Pre-flight Setup Checks ==="
23	
24	# ---- 1. API reachability ----
25	echo ""
26	echo "--- API Reachability ---"
27	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" --max-time 5 \
28	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
29	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
30	    -d '{"username":"__probe__","password":"probeprobe"}' 2>/dev/null)
31	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
32	
33	if [ -z "$HTTP_CODE" ] || [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "000" ]; then
34	    echo "  FAIL: API is not reachable at $BASE_URL"
35	    echo ""
36	    echo "  Ensure services are running:"
37	    echo "    docker compose up -d"
38	    echo ""
39	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
40	    echo "=== Setup Checks Summary: $PASS passed, $FAIL failed ==="
41	    exit $FAIL
42	else
43	    echo "  PASS: API is reachable at $BASE_URL"
44	    PASS=$((PASS + 1))
45	fi
46	
47	# ---- 2. Seed users exist ----
48	echo ""
49	echo "--- Seed Users ---"
50	
51	check_user() {
52	    local username="$1" role="$2"
53	    local resp http_code body
54	
55	    resp=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
56	        -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
57	        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
58	        -d "{\"username\":\"$username\",\"password\":\"testpass123\"}")
59	    http_code=$(echo "$resp" | tail -1)
60	    body=$(echo "$resp" | head -n -1)
61	
62	    if [ "$http_code" = "200" ]; then
63	        echo "  PASS: User '$username' exists and password is correct"
64	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
65	    else
66	        echo "  FAIL: User '$username' missing or wrong password (HTTP $http_code)"
67	        echo "        Seed data is required. Run:"
68	        echo "          docker compose exec postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -f /dev/stdin < scripts/seed.sql"
69	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
70	    fi
71	}
72	
73	check_user "admin"    "administrator"
74	check_user "seller1"  "seller"
75	check_user "buyer1"   "buyer"
76	check_user "analyst1" "compliance_analyst"
77	
78	# ---- 3. Seed collectibles exist ----
79	echo ""
80	echo "--- Seed Collectibles ---"
81	
82	# Log in as buyer to query collectibles (any authenticated user can list)
83	COOKIE_JAR=$(mktemp)
84	curl -s -c "$COOKIE_JAR" \
85	    -X POST "$BASE_URL/api/auth/login" \
86	    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
87	    -d '{"username":"buyer1","password":"testpass123"}' > /dev/null 2>&1
88	
89	CSRF_TOKEN=$(grep csrf_token "$COOKIE_JAR" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $NF}')
90	
91	RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
92	    -H "X-CSRF-Token: $CSRF_TOKEN" \
93	    "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles" 2>/dev/null)
94	HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
95	BODY=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1)
96	
97	if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ]; then
98	    TOTAL=$(echo "$BODY" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('total_count',0))" 2>/dev/null)
99	    if [ "$TOTAL" -ge 3 ] 2>/dev/null; then
100	        echo "  PASS: Seed collectibles present (count=$TOTAL)"
101	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
102	    else
103	        echo "  FAIL: Expected >= 3 seed collectibles, found $TOTAL"
104	        echo "        Run: docker compose exec postgres psql -U ledgermint -d ledgermint -f /dev/stdin < scripts/seed.sql"
105	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
106	    fi
107	else
108	    echo "  FAIL: Could not list collectibles (HTTP $HTTP_CODE) — seed check skipped"
109	    FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
110	fi
111	
112	# Check specific collectible IDs used by other tests
113	for CID in "10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001" \
114	            "10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000002" \
115	            "10000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000003"; do
116	    RESPONSE=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" -b "$COOKIE_JAR" \
117	        -H "X-CSRF-Token: $CSRF_TOKEN" \
118	        "$BASE_URL/api/collectibles/$CID" 2>/dev/null)
119	    HTTP_CODE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | tail -1)
120	    if [ "$HTTP_CODE" = "200" ]; then
121	        TITLE=$(echo "$RESPONSE" | head -n -1 | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['collectible']['title'])" 2>/dev/null)
122	        echo "  PASS: Collectible $CID exists ($TITLE)"
123	        PASS=$((PASS + 1))
124	    else
125	        echo "  FAIL: Collectible $CID not found (HTTP $HTTP_CODE)"
126	        FAIL=$((FAIL + 1))
127	    fi
128	done
129	
130	rm -f "$COOKIE_JAR"
131	
132	# ---- Summary ----
133	echo ""
134	if [ "$FAIL" -gt 0 ]; then
135	    echo "  *** SETUP FAILED — remaining API tests will likely produce false failures ***"
136	    echo "  Fix the issues above and re-run."
137	fi

139	exit $FAIL
